name: Synopsys Coverity SAST API
what_it_does: Enterprise SAST (Static Application Security Testing) platform with REST API — enabling security teams to find security vulnerabilities, quality defects, and compliance violations in source code through deep interprocedural analysis, with support for C/C++, Java, C#, JavaScript, Python, Go, Kotlin, Swift, and 20+ languages, featuring a Connect web server for team collaboration and REST API for CI/CD integration and result management.
use_cases: Scanning C/C++ codebases for memory safety vulnerabilities via Coverity in enterprise CI/CD
Managing security defects via Coverity Connect REST API for enterprise AppSec programs
Integrating Coverity SAST results via API into security dashboards and ticketing systems
Enforcing code quality gates via Coverity in pipeline builds for release governance
Auditing embedded/safety-critical code via Coverity for automotive/aerospace compliance
not_for: SCA/open-source dependency scanning (use Black Duck for SCA — different Synopsys product)
Small teams or startups without enterprise AppSec budgets (very expensive)
Pure runtime security or dynamic testing (SAST only — static analysis)
best_when: An enterprise AppSec team needs the industry-leading SAST for C/C++ and safety-critical code — where Coverity's deep interprocedural analysis and low false-positive rate matter for embedded, automotive, or large-scale enterprise software.
avoid_when: You need affordable SAST (use Semgrep or Checkmarx), SCA, runtime protection, or prefer open-source tools.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth with Coverity Connect username and password. API key authentication also available (generated in user profile). Authorization: Basic base64(username:password) or Authorization: Bearer {api_key}. REST API at https://{coverity-connect-host}/api/v2/. On-premise deployment — your own server URL. Role-based access control. No OAuth2. No webhooks (polling required for scan completion).

pricing.notes: Originally Coverity Inc. (San Francisco). Founded 2002 by Stanford researchers. Acquired by Synopsys 2014 for $374M. Synopsys Software Integrity sold to Clearlake Capital/Francisco Partners 2024 (becoming separate company). Industry standard for C/C++ SAST. Used by: NASA, Boeing, major automotive OEMs. MISRA C/C++ compliance checking. DO-178C avionics standard support. ISO 26262 automotive ASIL. Low false-positive rate is key differentiator. Interprocedural data flow analysis. Build capture model (cov-build wraps compiler). Coverity Connect: web server for team collaboration. Polaris: cloud-hosted alternative. Support for 20+ languages including C/C++, Java, C#, JavaScript, Python, Go, Kotlin.

performance.rate_limits: On-premise — no external rate limits; analysis time scales with codebase size

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP Basic or API key. On-premise URL varies. RBAC. Enterprise pricing (very expensive). No webhooks — polling. Build capture required (cov-build). 20+ languages. No free tier. Synopsys → Clearlake 2024 transition.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Defect reads idempotent by CID (Coverity ID). Stream reads idempotent. Snapshot (scan result) reads idempotent by snapshot ID. Triage actions are idempotent by defect.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted Coverity Connect: base URL is your company's server (e.g., https://coverity.company.com)
Build capture required: cov-build wraps compiler to observe source — no CLI-only scanning
Analysis is async: submit → wait for analysis complete → fetch results via API
CID (Coverity ID) is the defect identifier — persists across builds for the same defect
Stream vs snapshot: stream is the project, snapshot is a point-in-time scan result
Sold by Synopsys to Clearlake Capital 2024 — now independent brand; check current support channels

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (self-hosted customer responsibility). HTTP Basic or Bearer token. RBAC for users. ISO27001 certified. Source code analyzed locally on-premise — sensitive IP stays in-house. DO-178C/ISO26262 compliance for safety-critical. On-premise deployment for air-gapped environments. Ownership change (Synopsys → Clearlake 2024) introduces vendor risk consideration.
